operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot Selection view shows robot details and selected robot highlighting
operational_knowledge_units.summary: In the Robot Selection overview, choosing the Robot option allows the user to select a robot to view robot details. The selected robot is visually indicated by a box around it.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "When Robot option is selected, select a robot to view robot details."
Slide text: "The robot selected will have a box around it showing its selected."
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot Selection overview UI behavior: when Robot option is selected, selecting a robot shows robot details. Selected robot is indicated by a box around it.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot tote text under robot ID indicates tote presence and robot removal condition
operational_knowledge_units.summary: If a robot has a tote, the tote appears under the robot ID. A tote label prefixed with a question mark indicates the robot has been removed but the tote remains. If a robot does not have a tote, the display shows layer_1.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot Id."
Slide text: "If the tote has a “?” before it, that means the robot has been removed but the tote has not."
Slide text: "If a robot doesn’t have a tote it will say layer_1"

relationships.target: robot removed but tote not removed

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot tote display behavior: tote appears under robot ID. A '?' before tote means robot removed but tote remains. If no tote is present, the display says layer_1.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Robot colors indicate robot connectivity, task, and fault status
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Robot color coding communicates status in the Robot Selection view: purple means robot disconnected, blue or flashing blue means robot is in a task or searching for label, green means robot is not in a task, and red means fault or low battery.
observed_evidence: Slide text: "Blue/Flashing Blue: robot in task/searching for label"
Slide text: "Green: robot not in a task"

relationships.target: robot in task or searching for label

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Robot color meanings in Robot Selection: purple = disconnected, blue/flashing blue = in task or searching for label, green = not in task, red = fault or low battery.

procedure_candidate_attempts.title: Select a robot in Robot Selection to view details
preconditions: Robot option is selected in the UI.
procedure_candidate_attempts.procedure_visual_summary: Overview slide describing Robot Selection behavior, selected robot highlighting, tote text under robot ID, and robot color meanings.

steps.instruction: With the Robot option selected, select a robot in the Robot Selection view.
steps.expected_outcome: The selected robot is highlighted with a box around it and robot details are shown.
steps.validation_check: Confirm a box appears around the chosen robot and robot details become visible.
steps.visual_region_hint: Robot Selection area containing robot icons/list and selected robot highlight box.

procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Navigation candidate from Robot Selection overview: with Robot option selected, select a robot to view robot details. Selected robot is shown with a box around it.
